titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 620

p.seg: Alexander by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of the whole of his land greeting.

Know us in accordance with law to have received the monastery of Coldingham, the prior and convent of the same monastery, their men and lands, and all the possessions of the same, under our firm peace and protection. Wherefore do we strictly forbid that anyone should venture to bring about any evil, injury, annoyance or grievance to them, on pain of our full forfeiture.
As witnesses, Master Richard of Inverkeithing, chamberlain, Robert of Menzies, William de la Haye, and Geoffrey del Bois,
at Tynninghame, the 8th day of April, in the year of the reign of the lord king, the thirty-fifth [1249].
